response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
As someone who's been using social media for years, I've seen a lot of fake stuff. It can be really frustrating when you're scrolling through your feed and come across misleading posts or outright lies. But from my experience, there are ways to handle it without getting too stressed out.

I think the first thing is to stay aware that not everything online is true. I've got a moderate level of open-mindedness, so I try not to take things at face value right away. For example, when I see something suspicious on my feed, I don't just believe it because an influencer or someone with many followers posted it.

Another thing that helps me is checking multiple sources. Sometimes fake content goes viral because people retweet without verifying facts. Being employed as a delivery driver keeps my mind busy enough to not get bogged down by social media debates all day, but when I'm off work and watching some cinema or birdwatching – those are hobbies of mine that help me relax.

I also use tools like fact-checking websites in DC where we're surrounded by news outlets. It's good for someone with low agreeableness scores to not let fake content dictate their mood too much, but sometimes it still bothers me because I'm dealing with depression and Islam – my beliefs make authenticity really important.

Finally, setting boundaries is key. I don't spend all day on social media; that might be bad if I have high neuroticism levels or low conscientiousness like some people do. But for me, keeping time limits helps manage the stress from fake content.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To deal with fake content effectively and maintain mental well-being despite my personal struggles including depression, I suggest staying aware of misinformation, cross-referencing sources due to moderate open-mindedness levels in my Ocean personality trait, using fact-checkers as available in DC where I live, not letting it affect mood by keeping time limits thanks to low agreeableness scores reducing emotional absorption, and managing expectations given vocational education level limiting deep engagement capacity.
response_parsed.tone: Conversational yet concerned, moderate formal tone with personal anecdotes woven throughout
